reference_scope: aMule runtime behavior; completed ed2k-refactor tracker
current_surface: The completed ed2k-refactor tracker verifies protocol alignment, while the AR00 audit shows the next gap is runtime behavior: source maintenance, server/Kad cadence, peer lifecycle, queue reask, LowID state, identity, transfer pacing, upload cooperation, and credits.
target_or_prune_reason: Use aMule as the primary runtime behavior reference and use the completed refactor tracker as the local protocol baseline.
user_impact: Future work focuses on practical download reliability instead of repeating protocol-parity claims.

reference_scope: aMule user hash and credit identity persistence
current_surface: Current ED2K client identity must be audited against session/control-file persistence.
target_or_prune_reason: Persist or restore ED2K identity through aria2-next-native state so remote queues and credits are not reset unnecessarily.
user_impact: Peers can recognize the client across restarts where supported.

reference_scope: aMule known file, part file, server, Kad, and client runtime state
current_surface: aria2-next hidden session state and control files
target_or_prune_reason: Keep aria2-next ownership and store only runtime metadata needed for reliable scheduling, source discovery, identity, and credits.
user_impact: No legacy database stack is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule source limits, rare-file behavior, and source quality handling
target_or_prune_reason: Add explicit source caps and quality signals aligned with aria2-next scheduling instead of blindly connecting every known endpoint.
user_impact: Large source lists stop causing noisy or low-value connection churn.

reference_scope: aMule DeadSourceList and source removal rules
target_or_prune_reason: Add expiry-based dead-source state for failed, no-file, queue-full, unreachable, and unusable sources.
user_impact: Bad sources stop being re-added and retried too aggressively.

reference_scope: aMule no-needed-parts source state
target_or_prune_reason: Represent peers that are valid but temporarily useless separately from dead or failed peers.
user_impact: Useful peers are not discarded, and useless peers do not consume active slots.

reference_scope: aMule server TCP and UDP reask timing
current_surface: Ed2kCommand server path and Ed2kKadCommand server UDP path
target_or_prune_reason: Replace noisy polling with bounded per-file and per-server source request timing.
user_impact: Servers are queried responsibly and source refresh becomes predictable.

reference_scope: aMule Server and ServerList metadata
target_or_prune_reason: Use server UDP flags, source limits, user/file counts, and failure state to choose source requests.
user_impact: Server discovery uses the best available compatible request form.

reference_scope: aMule Kad routing and bootstrap maintenance
target_or_prune_reason: Maintain Kad routing state through bounded bootstrap, refresh, ping, and stale-contact expiry.
user_impact: Kad can become a durable source channel instead of a one-shot helper.

reference_scope: aMule Kad source search and source publish loops
target_or_prune_reason: Schedule Kad source search and publish based on file/source state and routing health.
user_impact: Low-source files get continued Kad discovery when Kad is usable.

reference_scope: aMule CUpDownClient download states
target_or_prune_reason: Introduce explicit peer state transitions for connect, queue, download, callback wait, no-needed-parts, dead, retry, no-file, and cancel.
user_impact: RPC status and scheduler behavior become easier to reason about.

reference_scope: aMule DownloadQueue and PartFile processing
target_or_prune_reason: Make policy choose the next peer action instead of only the next connectable peer.
user_impact: The runtime can reask, wait, retry, callback, or connect based on state.

reference_scope: aMule UDPReaskForDownload, UDPReaskACK, UDPReaskFNF
current_surface: Ed2kKadCommand OP_REASKACK parser and PeerState queue fields
target_or_prune_reason: Send periodic OP_REASKFILEPING to queued peers and update queue rank, part status, pending state, and failures from replies.
user_impact: Queued sources can advance instead of sitting at zero bytes indefinitely.

reference_scope: aMule UDP queue-full and file-not-found handling
target_or_prune_reason: Handle queue-full and file-not-found differently when evidence shows different retry horizons are needed.
user_impact: The client avoids both permanent source loss and tight retry loops.

reference_scope: aMule server callback and firewalled source handling
target_or_prune_reason: Track callback requested, accepted, failed, timed out, and impossible states explicitly.
user_impact: LowID sources do not poison normal direct-connect scheduling.

reference_scope: aMule Kad buddy and direct callback behavior
current_surface: No complete aria2-next owner is currently confirmed
target_or_prune_reason: Implement only if required for practical non-pruned download behavior; otherwise record a bounded limitation with evidence.
user_impact: LowID limitations are accurate and not hidden behind false capability claims.

reference_scope: aMule block request timing and timeout handling
target_or_prune_reason: Align request windows, timeout, cancel, range release, and retry behavior with aria2-next transfer ownership.
user_impact: Stalled peers stop holding useful ranges forever.

reference_scope: aMule corrupt block and stalled transfer recovery
target_or_prune_reason: Keep existing disk safety and add runtime recovery only where stalls or failed ranges need clearer scheduling state.
user_impact: Valid data remains safe while failed peers recover predictably.

reference_scope: aMule upload queue and reask response behavior
target_or_prune_reason: Keep cooperative upload responses aligned with download reask behavior and truthful local capabilities.
user_impact: Remote peers can interact with aria2-next as a useful ED2K participant.

reference_scope: aMule ClientCredits and secure-ident limits
target_or_prune_reason: Persist practical credit counters while keeping secure-ident unadvertised until fully implemented.
user_impact: Credit behavior improves fairness without false identity verification claims.

reference_scope: aMule wx UI, web UI, EC remote control, chat, friends, comments, preview, collections
target_or_prune_reason: Prune legacy application surfaces that do not belong in aria2-next. They are application-shell features, not download-runtime behavior required for reliable ED2K transfers.
user_impact: Scope stays focused on download reliability.

reference_scope: aMule UPnP/NAT helpers and port tests
target_or_prune_reason: Replace with existing aria2-next listen-port and documented firewall behavior unless a native owner is explicitly approved later.
user_impact: No extra NAT dependency or background router control is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule peer capability negotiation; completed ed2k-refactor capability ledger
target_or_prune_reason: Advertise only features with complete send, receive, state, error, and fallback paths after hardening changes.
user_impact: Peers do not select unsupported flows.

reference_scope: aria2-next RPC patterns and Motrix Next integration
target_or_prune_reason: Expose hardened runtime state only through stable ED2K fields and document remaining limitations.
user_impact: Integrators can distinguish waiting, queued, LowID, dead-source, and retry conditions.
